_description: Sample: Micro-Topic Importance Explanation — Law of Independent Assortment, NEET 2025, Student persona

insights.title: 🔴 CRITICAL: Law of Independent Assortment — 3–4 direct marks in NEET 2025
insights.claim: Law of Independent Assortment is a near-certain NEET 2025 topic with 92% predicted probability of appearance, supported by 9/10 years of historical exam data. You must revise this before any other Genetics topic.
insights.narrative:
Here's why this topic is your #1 priority in Genetics:

📊 PREDICTION SIGNAL: The PRAJNA engine gives this a 92% importance probability — that means out of 100 simulated NEET 2025 papers, 92 include at least one question from this micro-topic. The confidence in this prediction is 88%, which is high.

📅 HISTORY: This topic appeared in 9 out of the last 10 NEET papers. The one year it didn't appear directly (2019), it was tested indirectly through a dihybrid cross problem.

📈 TREND: The signal is RISING (+0.18 increase in 2024 vs 2023). NEET is asking more Genetics questions, not fewer.

🎯 WHAT TO EXPECT: Questions typically test:
• Predicting phenotype ratios in dihybrid crosses (most common)
• Identifying when Independent Assortment does NOT apply (linked genes — this is a trap!)
• Applying the law to explain genetic diversity in offspring

⏱️ STUDY EFFORT: Allocate 4 focused hours. After 2 hours of theory, spend 2 hours solving past NEET questions from 2015–2024 on this topic.

⚠️ RISK IF SKIPPED: Missing this topic risks 3–4 direct marks plus another 4–6 marks on related Chromosomal Theory questions that build on this concept.
insights.recommended_action: Spend 4 hours on Law of Independent Assortment this week: 2h theory (Mendel's dihybrid cross + ratio derivation) + 2h solving NEET PYQs from 2015–2024. Focus on linked gene exceptions — that's where NEET traps students.

evidence.excerpt: importance_probability=0.92, recurrence_score=0.90, historical_frequency=0.90 (9/10 years), trend_score=+0.18, confidence=0.88
